root_text.sn56.44:1.2: ‘ahaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ anabhisamecca …pe… dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ anabhisamecca sammā dukkhassantaṁ karissāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.44:2.2: ‘ahaṁ kūṭāgārassa heṭṭhimaṁ gharaṁ akaritvā uparimaṁ gharaṁ āropessāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.44:2.4: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yo evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn56.44:2.5: ‘ahaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ anabhisamecca …pe… dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ anabhisamecca sammā dukkhassantaṁ karissāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.44:3.1: Yo ca kho, bhikkhave, evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn56.44:3.2: ‘ahaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamecca …pe… dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamecca sammā dukkhassantaṁ karissāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.44:4.2: ‘ahaṁ kūṭāgārassa heṭṭhimaṁ gharaṁ karitvā uparimaṁ gharaṁ āropessāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.44:4.4: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yo evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn56.44:4.5: ‘ahaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamecca …pe… dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamecca sammā dukkhassantaṁ karissāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.44:5.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.44:1.2: ‘Without truly comprehending the noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path, I will completely make an end of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.44:2.1: It’s as if someone were to say:
translation_text.sn56.44:2.2: ‘Before the lower story of a bungalow is built, I will hoist up the upper story.’
translation_text.sn56.44:2.4: In the same way, suppose someone were to say:
translation_text.sn56.44:2.5: ‘Without truly comprehending the noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path, I will completely make an end of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.44:3.1: But suppose someone were to say:
translation_text.sn56.44:3.2: ‘After truly comprehending the noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path, I will completely make an end of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.44:4.1: It’s as if someone were to say:
translation_text.sn56.44:4.2: ‘After the lower story of a bungalow is built, I will hoist up the upper story.’
translation_text.sn56.44:4.4: In the same way, suppose someone were to say:
translation_text.sn56.44:4.5: ‘After truly comprehending the noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path, I will completely make an end of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.44:5.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”

variant_text.sn56.44:1.1: hi, bhikkhave → yo ca kho bhikkhave (sya-all, mr)
